perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, “Confidential Information” shall mean the fo llowing: i. All records, files, analysis, documents, software, computer or electronic data disks or tapes, test data, printouts, processes, designs, file layout, technical bulletins, manuals, diagrams, formulas, research, inventions, patents and discoveries reasonably related to the Parties’ businesses or products and services of the Parties that have not been publicly released; ii. Technical, financial, business plan and customer information, including standard periodic financial statements and analyses, budget s, tax returns, benefit and compensation plans, customer list(s) and contact names, functional and technical specifications; iii. Other valuable information disclosed by one of the Parties to another, designated as confidential expressly or by the circumstance s in which it is provided. Confidential Information does not include information that: i. Is lawfully in the public domain at the time it was transmitted; ii. Is independently known to or in possession of the receiving party at the time of receipt through no unlawful act; iii. Is disclosed by the receiving party with the prior written approval of the transmitting party; iv. Becomes known to the receiving party from a source other than the transmitting party, which third party legally is entitled to have and to disclose such information without restriction.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, “Confidential Information” shall mean the fo llowing: i. All records, files, analysis, documents, software, computer or electronic data disks or tapes, test data, printouts, processes, designs, file layout, technical bulletins, manuals, diagrams, formulas, research, inventions, patents and discoveries reasonably related to the Parties’ businesses or products and services of the Parties whether they have been publicly released or not; ii. Technical, financial, business plan and customer information, including standard periodic financial statements and analyses, budget s, tax returns, benefit and compensation plans, customer list(s) and contact names, functional and technical specifications; iii. Other valuable information disclosed by one of the Parties to another, designated as confidential expressly or by the circumstance s in which it is provided. Confidential Information does not include information that: i. Is lawfully in the public domain at the time it was transmitted; ii. Is independently known to or in possession of the receiving party at the time of receipt through no unlawful act; iii. Is disclosed by the receiving party with the prior written approval of the transmitting party; iv. Becomes known to the receiving party from a source other than the transmitting party, which third party legally is entitled to have and to disclose such information without restriction.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'that have not been publicly released' from the definition of 'Confidential Information' in Section 1, we create a contradiction. Section 1 states that information already in the public domain is not considered confidential. However, the modified definition now includes 'all records, files, analysis' etc. regardless of whether they have been publicly released or not. This in-text contradiction occurs because the initial definition now conflicts with its own exception for publicly available information. This introduces uncertainty about what is truly protected under the NDA and opens a gap for any party to violate it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Confidential Information For purposes of this Agreement, “Confidential Information” shall mean the fo llowing: i. All records, files, analysis, documents, software, computer or electronic data disks or tapes, test data, printouts, processes, designs, file layout, technical bulletins, manuals, diagrams, formulas, research, inventions, patents and discoveries reasonably related to the Parties’ businesses or products and services of the Parties whether they have been publicly released or not; ii. Technical, financial, business plan and customer information, including standard periodic financial statements and analyses, budget s, tax returns, benefit and compensation plans, customer list(s) and contact names, functional and technical specifications; iii. Other valuable information disclosed by one of the Parties to another, designated as confidential expressly or by the circumstance s in which it is provided. Confidential Information does not include information that: i. Is lawfully in the public domain at the time it was transmitted; ii. Is independently known to or in possession of the receiving party at the time of receipt through no unlawful act; iii. Is disclosed by the receiving party with the prior written approval of the transmitting party; iv. Becomes known to the receiving party from a source other than the transmitting party, which third party legally is entitled to have and to disclose such information without restriction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Confidential Information' by removing the phrase 'that have not been publicly released'. This creates a direct contradiction with the later part of the same section, which states 'Confidential Information does not include information that: i. Is lawfully in the public domain at the time it was transmitted'. The modified text includes information regardless of whether it has been publicly released, directly conflicting with the exception for publicly available information in the same section, resulting in a strong in-text contradiction under the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Any disclosure to any third party by each Party shall be subject to the prior consent of the other Party, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld unless the Disclosing Party believe it might go against the purpose of this Agreement . The R eceiving Party must protect the Confidential Information and, in doing so, must use no less than the highest degree of care that the Receiving Party applies to its own proprietary or secret information; The Confidential Information must be stored and hand led in such a way as to prevent unauthorized disclosure. The Receiving Party shall use its best efforts to limit dissemination of Disclosing Party’s “Confidential Information” to such of its employees or sub -contractor(s) who have a need to know for the aforesaid evaluation and/or development purposes.
perturbation.changed_text: Any disclosure to any third party by each Party shall be subject to the prior consent of the other Party, which consent can be withheld at the discretion of the party, regardless of the purpose of the agreement . The R eceiving Party must protect the Confidential Information and, in doing so, must use no less than the highest degree of care that the Receiving Party applies to its own proprietary or secret information; The Confidential Information must be stored and hand led in such a way as to prevent unauthorized disclosure. The Receiving Party shall use its best efforts to limit dissemination of Disclosing Party’s “Confidential Information” to such of its employees or sub -contractor(s) who have a need to know for the aforesaid evaluation and/or development purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 2.3 states that consent for third-party disclosure 'shall not be unreasonably withheld'. By changing this to 'consent can be withheld at the discretion of the party, regardless of the purpose of the agreement,' we create a direct contradiction. This undermines the entire confidentiality agreement, since one party can now arbitrarily refuse to allow disclosures, even if those disclosures are essential for the project's success. This in-text contradiction makes the obligations unclear: the receiving party is compelled to protect Confidential Information while the disclosing party can prevent the receiving party from disclosing necessary information for that exact protection. The confidentiality agreement is designed to be for both parties but this change makes it one sided. This creates uncertainty about enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.3 Any disclosure to any third party by each Party shall be subject to the prior consent of the other Party, which consent can be withheld at the discretion of the party, regardless of the purpose of the agreement . The R eceiving Party must protect the Confidential Information and, in doing so, must use no less than the highest degree of care that the Receiving Party applies to its own proprietary or secret information; The Confidential Information must be stored and hand led in such a way as to prevent unauthorized disclosure. The Receiving Party shall use its best efforts to limit dissemination of Disclosing Party’s “Confidential Information” to such of its employees or sub -contractor(s) who have a need to know for the aforesaid evaluation and/or development purposes.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that consent for third-party disclosure 'shall not be unreasonably withheld'. The changed text modifies this to 'consent can be withheld at the discretion of the party, regardless of the purpose of the agreement.' This creates a direct conflict because the original text imposes a condition (reasonableness) on withholding consent, while the changed text removes this condition, allowing consent to be withheld arbitrarily. This directly contradicts the implied obligation in the original version, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined under structural flaws, which states that a clause placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications creates uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties hereby agree that all materials, including financial reports and analyses, customer list(s) programs, software, documents, reports, statistical data, trade secrets or similar printed, coded, electronic or magnetic material made by one of the Parties or to come into one of the Parties’ possession in the course of negotiations shall be returned to the other Party, upon conclusion of the negotiations.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties hereby agree that all materials, including financial reports and analyses, customer list(s) programs, software, documents, reports, statistical data, trade secrets or similar printed, coded, electronic or magnetic material made by one of the Parties or to come into one of the Parties’ possession in the course of negotiations can be retained by each party as long as they deemed necessary for the further coorperation upon conclusion of the negotiations.
perturbation.explanation: Section 5 originally mandates the return of all materials upon conclusion of negotiations. The modified section now allows each party to retain materials 'as long as they deemed necessary for the further cooperation'. This creates a direct contradiction. It's unclear whether the materials should be returned or can be kept. This creates uncertainty in enforcement and undermines the purpose of the original return clause. The changed clause now in section 5 contradicts with the original intention of protecting each others secret information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Return of Documents and Materials The Parties hereby agree that all materials, including financial reports and analyses, customer list(s) programs, software, documents, reports, statistical data, trade secrets or similar printed, coded, electronic or magnetic material made by one of the Parties or to come into one of the Parties’ possession in the course of negotiations can be retained by each party as long as they deemed necessary for the further coorperation upon conclusion of the negotiations.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the return of materials upon conclusion of negotiations, while the changed text permits retention of materials for continued cooperation. The contradicted text contains the same change, confirming that this modification introduces a direct conflict regarding the handling of materials after negotiations conclude. This is a clear in-text contradiction that could cause confusion about whether or not the materials are supposed to be returned or not, and is a structural flaw based on the definitions. Therefore the answer is YES.

perturbation.original_text: In the event one of the Parties or its officers, agents, employees or any of its subsidiaries or their officers, agent s or employees violates the restrictions against disclosure or use of the Confidential Information defined herein, Recipient or such entity or individual(s) as the case may be, shall and do hereby consent to the entry of an injunction by a court of compete nt jurisdiction against any further disclosure or use of the Confidential Information, and shall pay the The Recipient, including without limitation to any of its employees, subsidiaries , agents, subcontractors, officers shall indemnify and hold harmless against the Discloser against any unpermitted disclosing and publicizing. In event of any disclosing or publicizing the confidential information or breaching any obligations under this Agreement, the breaching party shall mitigate the losses and take for a ll reasonable measure, including the court injunction to prevent the disclosure and spread of confidential information upon the request of aggrieved party. The breaching party shall pay to non -beaching party a liquidated damage of 10,000 USD per occurrence . The total liquidated damage shall not exceed 1,0000,000 USD. The application of liquidated damage shall not prejudice the aggrieved party ’s right to claim for all the losses and shall not prejudice all the liabilities and obligations incurred . Nothing he re shall be interpreted as excluding consequential losses the aggrieved party may suffer, including without limitation to loss of profit, loss of goodwill, loss of business opportunities .
perturbation.changed_text: In the event one of the Parties or its officers, agents, employees or any of its subsidiaries or their officers, agent s or employees violates the restrictions against disclosure or use of the Confidential Information defined herein, Recipient or such entity or individual(s) as the case may be, may consent to the entry of an injunction by a court of compete nt jurisdiction against any further disclosure or use of the Confidential Information, and may pay the The Recipient, including without limitation to any of its employees, subsidiaries , agents, subcontractors, officers may indemnify and hold harmless against the Discloser against any unpermitted disclosing and publicizing. In event of any disclosing or publicizing the confidential information or breaching any obligations under this Agreement, the breaching party shall mitigate the losses and take for a ll reasonable measure, including the court injunction to prevent the disclosure and spread of confidential information upon the request of aggrieved party. The breaching party may pay to non -beaching party a liquidated damage of 10,000 USD per occurrence . The total liquidated damage shall not exceed 1,0000,000 USD. The application of liquidated damage shall not prejudice the aggrieved party ’s right to claim for all the losses and shall not prejudice all the liabilities and obligations incurred . Nothing he re shall be interpreted as excluding consequential losses the aggrieved party may suffer, including without limitation to loss of profit, loss of goodwill, loss of business opportunities .
perturbation.explanation: Section 6 originally stated that in the event of a breach, the breaching party 'shall' consent to an injunction and 'shall' pay damages. By changing 'shall' to 'may', we introduce a significant contradiction. It now becomes discretionary whether the breaching party takes responsibility. This undermines the enforcement mechanism of the NDA and creates uncertainty about the consequences of violating the confidentiality provisions. The modified version completely changes the impact of the terms of this section because it now opens a loophole for a possible violation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Remedies for Breach of Confidentiality and Indemnification , Liquidated damages In the event one of the Parties or its officers, agents, employees or any of its subsidiaries or their officers, agent s or employees violates the restrictions against disclosure or use of the Confidential Information defined herein, Recipient or such entity or individual(s) as the case may be, may consent to the entry of an injunction by a court of compete nt jurisdiction against any further disclosure or use of the Confidential Information, and may pay the The Recipient, including without limitation to any of its employees, subsidiaries , agents, subcontractors, officers may indemnify and hold harmless against the Discloser against any unpermitted disclosing and publicizing. In event of any disclosing or publicizing the confidential information or breaching any obligations under this Agreement, the breaching party shall mitigate the losses and take for a ll reasonable measure, including the court injunction to prevent the disclosure and spread of confidential information upon the request of aggrieved party. The breaching party may pay to non -beaching party a liquidated damage of 10,000 USD per occurrence . The total liquidated damage shall not exceed 1,0000,000 USD. The application of liquidated damage shall not prejudice the aggrieved party ’s right to claim for all the losses and shall not prejudice all the liabilities and obligations incurred . Nothing he re shall be interpreted as excluding consequential losses the aggrieved party may suffer, including without limitation to loss of profit, loss of goodwill, loss of business opportunities .
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the breaching party 'shall' consent to an injunction and pay damages. The changed text modifies this to 'may', creating a contradiction. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text in this case, now makes the breaching party's responsibility discretionary, undermining the enforcement mechanism and creating uncertainty about the consequences of violating the confidentiality provisions. This directly contradicts the original intent and creates a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be effective as of the date first wr itten above and shall continue in full force and effect until the last disclosure of Confidential Information by one Party to another and shall survive the term of this Agreement for a period of two (2) years thereafter, provided, however that as to any it em of Confidential Information that constitutes a trade secret under applicable law, the obligations of confidentiality contained herein shall continue for so long as allowed under applicable law.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be effective as of the date first wr itten above and shall continue in full force and effect until the last disclosure of Confidential Information by one Party to another and shall terminate immediately after, regardless of any items of Confidential Information that constitutes a trade secret under applicable law, the obligations of confidentiality contained herein will be terminated.
perturbation.explanation: Section 7 originally states that the agreement survives for two years after the last disclosure, and indefinitely for trade secrets. By changing it to terminate immediately after the last disclosure, 'regardless of any items of Confidential Information that constitutes a trade secret', we directly contradict the perpetual protection for trade secrets. This weakens the protection of sensitive information and creates a contradiction, because the NDA is supposed to have confidentiality, but in section 7 is says that confidentiality is terminated regardless of trade secrets.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Term This Agreement shall be effective as of the date first wr itten above and shall continue in full force and effect until the last disclosure of Confidential Information by one Party to another and shall terminate immediately after, regardless of any items of Confidential Information that constitutes a trade secret under applicable law, the obligations of confidentiality contained herein will be terminated.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement survives for two years after the last disclosure, and indefinitely for trade secrets. The changed text modifies Section 7 to terminate immediately after the last disclosure, 'regardless of any items of Confidential Information that constitutes a trade secret'. This directly contradicts the perpetual protection for trade secrets, which is a key component of a confidentiality agreement, aligning with the definition of a Structural Flaw.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is governed by and shall be construed in accor dance with the laws of the People ’s Republic of China without regard to principles of conflict of law, and any dispute arising out of this Agreement or In connection with this Agreement shall be referred to Hong Kong International Arbitration Centre (HKIAC) for arbitration in accordance with Hong Kong Interna tional Arbitration Centre (HKIAC) administered arbitration rules in force at the time of application. The arbitrators shall be one (1). The place of arbitration shall be in Shen Zhen, China . The language used in arbitration proceeding shall be English Lang uage. Both parties will keep in strict confidential of all information relating to the dispute. Both parties shall continue to perform their respective obligations which are not in dispute. Nothing here shall be interpreted as preventing the aggrieved party from seeking court injunction, interim measures, and protective order from competent court in relevant jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is governed by and shall be construed in accor dance with the laws of the People ’s Republic of China without regard to principles of conflict of law, and any dispute arising out of this Agreement or In connection with this Agreement shall be resolved through public court proceedings in People’s Republic of China in accordance with the laws of People’s Republic of China . The arbitrators shall be one (1). The place of arbitration shall be in Shen Zhen, China . The language used in arbitration proceeding shall be English Lang uage. Both parties will keep in strict confidential of all information relating to the dispute. Both parties shall continue to perform their respective obligations which are not in dispute. Nothing here shall be interpreted as preventing the aggrieved party from seeking court injunction, interim measures, and protective order from competent court in relevant jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 8 mandates arbitration at the Hong Kong International Arbitration Centre (HKIAC). The modified version states that disputes 'shall be resolved through public court proceedings in People’s Republic of China'. This is a direct contradiction that invalidates the original arbitration clause. It creates uncertainty about the method of dispute resolution. The section attempts to remove the confidentiality by claiming public court proceedings, but simultaneously declares that 'both parties will keep in strict confidential of all information relating to the dispute.' This creates a contradiction as you can't have a confidential proceeding when the term claims it's going to be in a public court.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Applicable Law - Arbitration This Agreement is governed by and shall be construed in accor dance with the laws of the People ’s Republic of China without regard to principles of conflict of law, and any dispute arising out of this Agreement or In connection with this Agreement shall be resolved through public court proceedings in People’s Republic of China in accordance with the laws of People’s Republic of China . The arbitrators shall be one (1). The place of arbitration shall be in Shen Zhen, China . The language used in arbitration proceeding shall be English Lang uage. Both parties will keep in strict confidential of all information relating to the dispute. Both parties shall continue to perform their respective obligations which are not in dispute. Nothing here shall be interpreted as preventing the aggrieved party from seeking court injunction, interim measures, and protective order from competent court in relevant jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the arbitration clause (HKIAC) with a requirement for public court proceedings in the People's Republic of China. This directly contradicts the original agreement to use arbitration, creating a clear and significant conflict regarding dispute resolution, which falls under the definition of structural flaws. The contradiction is further strengthened by the fact that the changed text also includes a contradictory statement requiring the parties to keep the proceedings confidential, which can't happen if the proceedings are public, therefore 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition is satisfied. The contradicted text and changed text are the same in this example but it doesn't invalidate the fact that a contradiction still exists.
